overview: Temur Prowess is a fast, artifact-centric strategy that leverages the prowess mechanic to create unblockable, oversized threats. By cycling cheap creatures alongside low-cost artifacts, the deck applies immediate pressure while maintaining board presence. The U/R/G color identity provides efficient removal, card advantage, and flexible mana to outpace midrange and control shells.
win_conditions: Combat damage from unblockable prowess creatures
Overwhelming board presence with artifact synergies
Fast clock that forces opponents into unfavorable trades

keep_criteria: Hands with at least one artifact and a one-drop prowess creature
Two-card starts featuring Mishra's Bauble and early aggression
Draws with early removal and artifact enablers
Keep hands with 2-3 lands (preferably fetch/shock-compatible), at least one 1-mana spell (Mishra's Bauble, Opt, Lightning Strike), and a Prowess threat (Slickshot Show-Off or Cori-Steel Cutter).
Prioritize hands that enable a turn-1 Bauble or cantrip into a turn-2 threat.
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking artifacts or early creature drops
Slow draws heavy on late-game cards or high mana costs
Hands with no interaction against early board wipes
Mulligan on 2+ lands with no 1-drops, 3+ lands with no threats, or hands that cannot cast two spells on turn 1.

early_game: Play artifacts on curve to enable prowess immediately
Drop cheap creatures to establish early pressure and unblockability
Use early removal to clear opposing one-drops and protect your board
Turn 1: Play land, cast Mishra's Bauble or a 1-drop cantrip/inst.
Turn 2: Play land, cast another spell, then drop Slickshot Show-Off or Cori-Steel Cutter.
Sequence Bauble -> Opt -> Slickshot to maximize Prowess triggers before combat.
If opening with Cori-Steel Cutter, ensure you control a creature to cast it for 2, or sequence a 1-drop first to enable the discount.
mid_game: Ramp mana production with additional artifacts to play larger threats
Trade efficiently while maintaining at least one artifact on the battlefield
Apply constant combat pressure to force opponent's resources into inefficient answers
Prioritize card advantage over raw board presence.
Never cast a Prowess spell if it leaves you with zero cards and no threats unless necessary to stabilize.
Use Mishra's Bauble to dig for answers or threats, but avoid overextending into spot removal.
Keep mana open for interaction (Lightning Strike, Abrupt Decay) while sequencing spells to maximize Prowess triggers.
Commit threats when you have card advantage or interaction to protect them.
Hold back if the opponent has sweepers or spot removal ready.
late_game: Maintain artifact count to keep prowess active and threats unblockable
Use card draw and value generators to outlast resource-depleted opponents
Close out games with wide, unblockable attacks or oversized finishers
Slickshot Show-Off with 5+ Prowess triggers (7/7 flyer) or Cori-Steel Cutter with massive stat inflation.
Direct damage spells like Lightning Strike or Lightning Axe to close out games, trade up, or remove key blockers.
Use Prowess to stabilize into a lethal attack or force unfavorable trades.
Go all-in when you have card advantage, the opponent has no interaction, or you can trade threats efficiently.
Commit when you can cast 2+ spells this turn to guarantee a lethal-sized threat or force a favorable combat math scenario.
Don't hold back if you can pressure through their remaining interaction.

key_cards.name: Slickshot Show-Off
key_cards.notes: Play alongside artifacts to become unblockable and gain +1/+1 counters, applying immediate pressure while surviving early trades.

key_cards.name: Cori-Steel Cutter
key_cards.notes: Use its enter-the-battlefield effect to remove problematic opponent artifacts while simultaneously activating prowess for your other creatures.

key_cards.name: Mishra's Bauble
key_cards.role: Core artifact enabler and mana acceleration
key_cards.notes: Drop on turn one to immediately trigger prowess, then untap to fetch mana for larger threats or additional artifacts.

sequencing_tips: Hold artifact plays in hand until combat to surprise blockers and trigger prowess unexpectedly
Prioritize playing prowess creatures before opponent's blockers resolve to secure unblockable status
Sequence removal to clear threats that would otherwise remove your artifacts or key creatures
Manage artifact count carefully to ensure prowess remains active even after board wipes or targeted hate
Common mistakes include overcasting Prowess spells without threats on board, ignoring opponent interaction, and mismanaging Mishra's Bauble (sacrificing it too early or late).
Cori-Steel Cutter can be cast for 2 if you control a creature, making it a powerful early drop.
Slickshot's flying makes it difficult to block, so use it to pressure or trade efficiently.
Always sequence spells to maximize Prowess triggers before combat, and track graveyard hate if you rely on Thought Scour.

matchups.advice: Ramp aggressively and go wide to overwhelm slow answers, avoiding artifact-heavy plays if they run targeted hate.
Play artifacts early to enable prowess, use unblockable threats to bypass their board, and prioritize removal for their biggest creatures.
Race efficiently by maintaining constant pressure, trade favorably while keeping an artifact out, and punish slow starts with lethal sequences.

Cast cheap spells to trigger Prowess before casting threats. Drop threats at end step to avoid counters. Use Bauble to dig for answers or additional spells to trade up. Never overcommit to a single threat; use Prowess to force multiple blocks or trades.

Race them aggressively. Use cantrips to find interaction and disrupt their mana base. Don't let them stabilize; pressure early and often. Use Bauble to accelerate your own game plan and outpace their ramp spells.

Value card advantage and efficient trades. Use Lightning Axe to remove their threats and disrupt their Prowess triggers. Don't overcommit to Prowess if they have removal. Win by having more resources, better sequencing, and superior sideboard positioning.

common_threats: Watch for sweepers (Supreme Verdict, Boros Charm), spot removal (Lightning Axe, Abrupt Decay, Path to Exile), and combo win conditions.
Note if the opponent is playing tapped lands or artifact acceleration (Affinity).
Track graveyard hate if you rely on Thought Scour, and monitor their hand size to gauge interaction availability.

sequencing_tips_evidence.evidence_span: Prowess mechanic timing and artifact dependency

common_threats_evidence.evidence_span: Modern format meta and artifact/prowess synergy vulnerabilities
